conflict: Water, mud, and torn paper reduce years of lived history to fragile objects just as Halima realizes the missing pages point toward targeted manipulation rather than random chaos.
consequence: Halima identifies that copied ledger pages linked to Mofiz's land interest are gone, shifting the problem from diffuse fear to probable deliberate theft.
must_land_beats: The physical aftermath of the search and storm is rendered through objects, smells, and damage.
Halima and Nura work side by side without full emotional repair.
The missing pages are identified as specific, not generic, losses.
The chapter turns on recognition and inference rather than confrontation.
objective: Salvage damaged documents after the storm and determine what was actually taken in the search.
